task_completion.description: Create Page 1 titled 'Authorization Code Flow with PKCE' with the four required participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Draw.io interface, the current page tab or a prominent title should read 'Authorization Code Flow with PKCE'. There must be four UML lifelines (participants) labeled: User (or Resource Owner), Client, Authorization Server, and Resource Server.

task_completion.description: Model the Authorization Code Flow with PKCE on Page 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sequence message arrows between the lifelines showing: 1) an initial request containing 'code_challenge', 2) user login/consent steps, 3) an exchange containing 'code_verifier', and 4) data fetching using an access token.

task_completion.description: Include at least one self-message and a security note regarding PKCE on Page 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A looped message arrow originating and ending on the same lifeline (e.g., for internal verification) AND a distinct text box or note shape containing text about PKCE security.

task_completion.description: Create Page 2 titled 'Token Refresh Flow' modeling the refresh token exchange.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second page tab in Draw.io titled 'Token Refresh Flow'. The canvas for this page should show a sequence diagram depicting a refresh token being exchanged for a new access token.

task_completion.description: Save the sequence diagram as oauth2_sequence.drawio on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Draw.io window title bar shows 'oauth2_sequence.drawio' or a file manager window shows the file 'oauth2_sequence.drawio' located in the ~/Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: Export Page 1 as a PNG image to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window showing 'oauth2_sequence.png' on the Desktop, or an image viewer open showing the exported PNG which visually matches the Page 1 sequence diagram.

integrity.description: The agent used the Draw.io graphical user interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking, dragging shapes, and typing within the Draw.io application window, rather than generating the .drawio XML file entirely via terminal scripts or a text editor.

integrity.description: The diagram is constructed using proper UML sequence diagram elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram should consist of actual lifeline shapes, activation boxes, and connected message arrows, rather than being a single pasted image or plain text formatted to look like a diagram.

integrity.description: The PNG file is a genuine export of the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported oauth2_sequence.png should cleanly show the diagram canvas. It should not be a full-screen desktop screenshot manually renamed to .png.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are /home/ga/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.png. The diagram must contain exactly two pages: 'Authorization Code Flow with PKCE' and 'Token Refresh Flow'. Page 1 must include four participants: 'User' (or Resource Owner), 'Client', 'Authorization Server', and 'Resource Server'. The diagram text across the flow must include the authentic OAuth 2.0/PKCE keywords: 'code_challenge', 'code_verifier', 'access_token', 'refresh_token', 'bearer', and 'authorization code'. The total flow should depict at least 8 to 10 distinct message exchanges.
